properties.name: Hanifaru Bay, Maldives
properties.description: One of the largest known manta ray feeding aggregations on Earth. Up to 200+ individuals in a single tidal cycle during peak plankton blooms.

properties.name: Ari Atoll, Maldives
properties.description: Year-round resident reef manta population with multiple known cleaning stations. Well-studied individual ID dataset.

properties.name: Ningaloo Reef, Australia
properties.description: Large resident reef manta population; aggregations coincide with coral spawning and seasonal productivity pulses.

properties.name: Coral Bay, Australia
properties.description: Year-round manta presence south of Ningaloo; important juvenile habitat and cleaning station zone.

properties.name: Isla Holbox, Mexico
properties.description: Seasonal feeding aggregation of oceanic mantas in the Yucatán Channel, driven by upwelling-related plankton blooms.

properties.name: Revillagigedo Archipelago, Mexico
properties.description: Remote oceanic island group hosting large oceanic manta aggregations; cleaning behavior and courtship documented extensively.

properties.name: Bat Islands, Costa Rica
properties.description: Aggregation site for oceanic mantas in the Papagayo upwelling zone; one of the most accessible mainland Pacific hotspots.

properties.name: Cocos Island, Costa Rica
properties.description: Remote UNESCO World Heritage seamount with year-round oceanic manta presence alongside sharks and other megafauna.

properties.name: Pemba Island, Tanzania
properties.description: Resident reef manta population in the Zanzibar Archipelago; cleaning stations and feeding aggregations documented.
properties.citation: Rohner et al. (2013) — African Journal of Marine Science

properties.name: Tofo Beach, Mozambique
properties.description: One of the most important oceanic manta ray sites globally; large population with year-round presence and extensive photo-ID catalogue.

properties.name: Mahé, Seychelles
properties.description: Reef manta population on the Mahé Plateau; cleaning stations on inner granitic island reefs.

properties.name: Hamata, Egypt (Red Sea)
properties.description: Resident reef mantas at cleaning stations on outer Red Sea reefs near the Egyptian–Sudanese border.

properties.name: Port Sudan, Red Sea
properties.description: Reef mantas associated with Sha'ab Rumi and Sanganeb Atoll reef systems; aggregations at cleaning stations.

properties.name: Gulf of Tadjoura, Djibouti
properties.description: Seasonal oceanic manta aggregation driven by winter upwelling and zooplankton blooms in the Gulf of Tadjoura.
properties.citation: Rowat & Brooks (2012) — Journal of Fish Biology

properties.name: Komodo, Indonesia
properties.description: Reef manta feeding aggregations driven by upwelling in Komodo National Park; peak activity during SE monsoon.
properties.citation: Couturier et al. (2012) — Journal of Fish Biology

properties.name: Nusa Penida, Bali, Indonesia
properties.description: Highly accessible reef manta cleaning station in the Bali Strait; site of long-term population monitoring by Project Manta.
properties.citation: Germanov et al. (2019) — Frontiers in Marine Science

properties.name: Raja Ampat, Indonesia
properties.description: Coral Triangle hotspot for reef manta rays; multiple cleaning stations in the Bird's Head Seascape.

properties.name: Koh Bon, Thailand
properties.description: Seasonal oceanic manta aggregation in the Andaman Sea during the northeast monsoon; feeding on surface zooplankton.
properties.citation: Seah et al. (2019) — Ocean & Coastal Management

properties.name: Similan Islands, Thailand
properties.description: Oceanic manta aggregation at cleaning stations on exposed seamounts during the NE monsoon season.
properties.citation: Seah et al. (2019) — Ocean & Coastal Management

properties.name: Palau
properties.description: Year-round resident reef manta population at German Channel and other well-known cleaning stations.

properties.name: Yap, Micronesia
properties.description: Long-studied reef manta population at Mi'l Channel; one of the earliest sites for individual photo-ID research.

properties.name: Guam, Micronesia
properties.description: Resident reef manta rays at cleaning stations on the western reef slope; long-term monitoring by UOG Marine Lab.

properties.name: Keahole Point, Hawaii
properties.description: Iconic cleaning station aggregation off the Kona coast; night dives attract mantas to plankton drawn by resort lights.

properties.name: Oahu, Hawaii
properties.description: Resident reef manta population on the North Shore; cleaning stations at Sharks Cove and associated reefs.

properties.name: Fernando de Noronha, Brazil
properties.description: Seasonal oceanic manta aggregation at this remote Atlantic archipelago; feeding on productive upwelling-driven plankton.
properties.citation: Luiz et al. (2009) — Journal of the Marine Biological Association

properties.name: Azores, Portugal
properties.description: Seasonal oceanic manta aggregation in the mid-Atlantic; feeding on deep scattering layer organisms during summer upwelling.

properties.name: Flower Garden Banks, Gulf of Mexico
properties.description: Oceanic manta aggregation at offshore coral banks in the NW Gulf of Mexico; feeding on fish spawning aggregations.
properties.citation: Zimmerman & Ritchie (2006) — Gulf of Mexico Science

properties.name: Chagos Archipelago, BIOT
properties.description: Oceanic manta rays in one of the most pristine and remote marine environments on Earth; minimal human disturbance.
